DISCOVERY 612, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT RAMI SOUSOU, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, FOR EVIDENCE AND ISSUE SANCTIONS; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS IT SEEKS A TERMINATING SANCTION AND ENTRY OF DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST DEFENDANT RAMI SOUSOU. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT RAMI SOUSOU'S FAILURE TO RESPOND AND TO COMPLY WITH THE COURT'S ORDER TO RESPOND WERE A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS AND THAT FURTHER ORDERS TO RESPOND WOULD NOT RESULT IN COMPLIANCE. THE PROPOSED INJUNCTION AND JUDGMENT IS ADOPTED BY THE COURT. ORDER AND JUDGMENT SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Render Terminating Sanction Of Default Judgment Against Deft Rami Sousou, Or In The Alternative For Evidence And Issue Sanctions, And For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-02-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearance:
Jill L. Cannon, Esq., for City and County of San Francisco, Plaintiff/Moving Party

Tentative Ruling adopted: Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Of Default Judgment Against Defendant Rami Sousou, or in the alternative, For Evidence And Issue Sanctions; and For Monetary Sanctions is granted as it seeks a terminating sanction and entry of default judgment against Defendant Rami Sousou. The court finds that Defendant Rami Sousou's failure to respond and to comply with the court's order to respond were a misuse of the discovery process and that further orders to respond would not result in compliance. The proposed injunction and judgment is adopted by the court. Order and judgment signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-23-2006 Set For Trial On Apr-30-07 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-12-07 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 611

Ntc Of Motion And Motion To Compel Rami Sousou'S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Oct-10-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff City & County of San Francisco's Motion To Compel Defendant Rami Sousou's Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, And Request For Production Of Documents; And For An Order Establishing Admissions is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. The Requests for Admissions are deemed admitted. Defendant is ordered to respond to the Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories, and Request For Production of documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant Rami Sousou in the amount of $800, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-17-2006 Continued To Nov-03-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-20-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's Order to Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction.
APPEARANCE: Niall Vignoles, Deputy City Atty 554-3906 for pltf & moving party; Yano Rubinstein, Esq. of Rubenstein Law Group 277-1900 for deft & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court granted the preliminary injunction. Court retains jurisdiction. TRO stays in effect. Parties to attempt to agree on proposed form of order, otherwise each to submit its own. (301/JLW/DM) Judge: James L. Warren, Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558
